config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan সমাজ, karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum dan hak pribadi, bukan menempatkan yang satu sepenuhnya di atas yang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, although in China I have seen how people often accept some personal sacrifice for stability, order, and collective goals. That instinct makes sense to me in cases like public health, disaster response, or preventing chaos, but it becomes dangerous when "society" is used as a blanket excuse to silence people or ignore basic rights. A healthy society should protect the public interest without treating individual rights as disposable, because once the individual has no firm protection, the society itself can become unfair and brittle. So I see this as a balance, but with clear limits: collective needs matter, yet they should not erase dignity, due process, or personal freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal hak warga justru perlu jadi pagar agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti darurat kesehatan, bencana, atau keamanan publik yang menuntut pengorbanan individu, tetapi itu harus bersifat terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji. Jadi, yang benar menurut saya bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat, melainkan menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama dengan perlindungan hak individu yang tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in my view society’s interests should not sit above the individual’s rights by default. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, since those can prevent chaos and help everyone in practical ways, but I have also seen how easily “for society” can become a vague excuse to pressure ordinary people and weaken basic protections. In my opinion, a society is only truly strong when individual dignity, due process, and freedom are treated as real limits on collective power, not as obstacles to be overridden whenever convenient. Collective needs can justify temporary restrictions in genuine emergencies, but not a permanent hierarchy where the individual is expected to yield without question.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga dan menutup kritik. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana, wabah, atau ancaman keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan sementara, tetapi itu harus jelas batasnya, transparan, dan bisa diawasi. Kalau hak individu selalu dianggap lebih rendah, yang terjadi justru kekuasaan jadi leluasa dan masyarakat biasa yang paling mudah dirugikan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang menjaga kepentingan umum sambil tetap menegakkan hak dan martabat tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general principle, because from my perspective in China, putting society first too easily turns into a blank check for authority. I understand why many people here value stability, order, and collective goals, and I do think those matter in emergencies like epidemics or disasters, but they should be temporary and tightly limited. The problem is that once "society" is treated as always more important than the individual, ordinary people lose a real way to defend dignity, due process, and personal freedom. A strong society should protect the common good without making individual rights feel optional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
